title: eriogonum multiceps subsp. typicum
intro: Eriogonum multiceps subsp. typicum, also known as desert buckwheat or wild buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum multiceps subsp. typicum include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever and skin conditions.
